_comment: The neutral guardrail policy (Loom 2.0-rc.13 · WS4). The Loom is distributed as a Claude Code plugin marketplace, so its local pre-write guardrails (hooks/) are Claude Code-specific. This policy states, per guardrail and per runtime, EXACTLY what is enforced where — and where it is NOT — so the Loom never implies equivalent protection on a runtime that lacks the hook. States: `enforced` (a local mechanism blocks it at the point of action) · `ci-backstop` (no local block, but a CI gate catches it before merge — the enforcement of record) · `uncovered` (no mechanism; an honest gap, which must be acknowledged with known_gap:true if the decision is `block`). scripts/guardrail-policy-check.mjs verifies every enforced/ci-backstop state names an EXISTING mechanism and every uncovered state names none — no implied coverage. The capability matrix in README.md is generated from this file and gated by doc-integrity.

guardrails.description: PII-shaped literals (Emirates ID, IBAN) are blocked — synthetic data only

github-actions.note: catches the secret-shaped subset in tree + history; not a full PII scanner

local-git.note: no pre-commit hook shipped — wire pii-guard as a pre-commit hook to cover this runtime

guardrails.description: Test-disabling edits (it.skip/.only/.todo/.fails/xit, commented-out assertions) are blocked
The API contract cannot change inside a feature branch — spec changes take the spec lane

github-actions.note: the contract path is a CODEOWNERS-owned control target — a change requires Code Owner review

guardrails.description: The agent cannot edit its own guardrails (gates, workflows, governance data, CODEOWNERS)

claude-code.note: no local hook blocks control-plane writes — the agent can PROPOSE the edit; CI + Code Owner review is what prevents it merging

guardrails.description: Institutional BrainKit content cannot be altered by a builder without the context owner

claude-code.note: no local hook; institution/ is proposed like any file and blocked at merge

guardrails.description: Unauthorized network/data egress during a build or agent session

claude-code.note: Claude Code ships no default network-egress hook; use a sandbox/allowlist at the runtime boundary

github-actions.note: the bundle ships no egress gate; enforce with an egress-controlled runner (allowlist) or a network policy — adopter-side

guardrails.description: A guard that cannot parse its input (jq missing) must DENY, never silently pass

claude-code.note: all three hooks emit a deny decision when jq is absent (rc.10)

github-actions.note: CI gates run under Node with no jq dependency; a parse failure exits non-zero and blocks
